summary: Fei-Fei Li argues that the next major frontier in artificial intelligence is 'spatial intelligence.' Through her company World Labs, she is developing 'world models' that possess a physics-based understanding of 3D environments, which are essential for advancing fields like robotics that require interactable, dynamic training grounds.

principles.statement: Spatial intelligence is the next frontier of artificial intelligence.
principles.rationale: To achieve AI's biggest promises, systems must move beyond just modeling words and language to actually perceiving and understanding the physical 3D world.
principles.source_quote: We firmly believe that spatial intelligence is the next frontier of artificial intelligence,

principles.statement: Training real-world robots requires dynamic, physics-based 3D environments.
principles.rationale: Robots currently struggle with real-world utility; to evaluate and train them effectively, they need simulated environments that accurately reflect physical realities like collisions and dynamics.
principles.source_quote: You need a 3D environment that is interactable, that has collisions, physics, and dynamics to train and evaluate robots,

mental_models.name: World Models
mental_models.description: An AI paradigm that moves beyond language processing to develop a physics-based understanding of three-dimensional environments, allowing AI to perceive and interact with spatial data.
mental_models.example: World Labs' 'Marble' model, which generates realistic, interactable 3D worlds for game development and robotics training.

signature_quotes.text: We firmly believe that spatial intelligence is the next frontier of artificial intelligence,
signature_quotes.context: Discussing the need for AI to move beyond large language models.

signature_quotes.text: You need a 3D environment that is interactable, that has collisions, physics, and dynamics to train and evaluate robots,
signature_quotes.context: Explaining why spatial intelligence and world models are critical for the robotics industry.

anti_patterns.description: Relying exclusively on large language models (LLMs) to achieve artificial general intelligence or real-world utility.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: LLMs only develop a model of words and their meanings; they lack the ability to perceive the real world or understand spatial, physics-based 3D environments.
